reviews.review_description: AstroPark is a fun and varied VR experience that takes the space theme to the next level. Whether you're piloting a module through meteor showers or driving a lunar vehicle to restore solar power, each mission challenges different skills — from problem-solving to coordination. The free-movement environment adds to the immersion, and the game feels polished and engaging for all ages. What makes it even better? The studio continues to update and expand the game regularly, making it a great investment for both new and returning players. A must-have for space lovers in VR!

Playing this game in early access on the meta quest 3 was fun, immersive & educational. There were no bugs after completing all the parts they have available to play which is great considering its early access. The game was simple to understand and fun whilst also educational. I believe the target audience is more than likely children as it’s telling you facts as you solve the puzzles about space. But still is playable by all ages. I just know my son would love this game and I know other children would to. This game has a lot of potential and I know there is still more to come. Looking forward to the coming soon 🙃 Great job soup 🥹🚀
fun game with different space-related missions and minigames. Quite immersive, loved the music, and I'm excited to see new missions and content!

Astropark has a few fun, interactive and educational mini games to offer which are nicely integrated into the park. Loved cruising over the moon surface with the rover while learning about battery management in space. Playing with gravity in while throwing apples at targets is also a very clever and fun experience. Great job 👏🏼

Really loved being able to learn about gravity and Mars from the comfort of my couch! I was really impressed with this experience, very nice art and content. Also, available in Spanish and English, which is very rare for an AppLab game. There’s a lot of potential for growth!
Slow as in pacing. Graphics is jittery (due to lagging frames?) and sometimes very stuttery, on Quest 2. Educational content is for kids.

developer_response.body: Oh thank you so much for reviewing our game in development!

reviews.review_description: Astro Park VR dazzles with its vivid visuals, offering a sensory feast that transports you straight into the cosmos. The game's meticulous design details, from the shimmering asteroid belts to the mighty gas giants, all add to the immersion, making you feel like a true space explorer. The standout for me was navigating the gravitational anomalies, not only fun but also surprisingly educational, like a hands-on physics lesson. The sessions are succinct, yet they're densely packed with engaging activities that never fail to pique my curiosity. While I relished every moment, I did find myself wishing for a bit more control over my movement, which I hope future updates might address. Astro Park VR is a gem in the VR space, masterfully blending education with entertainment. I am eagerly awaiting more educational content; the potential for learning in this virtual universe is truly sky-high.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for this amazing review! We are glad that you liked it. More to come really soon!

reviews.review_title: Beautiful with a lot of potential!
reviews.review_description: I tried it on Quest 3 and it looked really colorful and beautiful.  I was amazingly surprised by the experiences but I hope there is a lot more to come in the future.  The traveling felt like I was using hyperdrive and the fact that I could choose the gravity in the Newton experience was awesome! But they were all really short.  Also, the fact that I couldn’t use the stick to move myself left and right was bothersome.  I hope that changes in the future.  But again, overall it was really fun and visually stunning!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review! more to come soon! We are also going to add the side movement.
